introduction:
A Death Certificate in Andhra Pradesh is issued by the local Municipality, Gram Panchayat, or Grama Sachivalayam under the Registration of Births and Deaths Act, 1969. It is the official legal proof of death and records the name, date, place, and cause of death.

The certificate is essential for claiming insurance, settling bank accounts, property mutation, pension claims, and legal succession proceedings. In AP, death registration is handled through MeeSeva centers and the CRS portal (crsorgi.gov.in).
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Death must be registered within 21 days of occurrence (free of charge)
  • Registration between 21 days and 1 year requires written permission from Registrar + late fee
  • Registration after 1 year requires Magistrate / RDO order
  • First certified copy is FREE; subsequent copies at nominal fee
  • Track application at crsorgi.gov.in or through MeeSeva
  • Helpline: 1800-425-2977 (Toll-Free)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Death Certificate

Note: Submit self-attested photocopies and carry original documents for verification.
Proof of Death
  • Medical Certificate of Cause of Death (MCCD) from hospital
  • Hospital discharge / death summary
  • Cremation / Burial ground receipt
  • FIR / Panchnama (for unnatural deaths)
Identity Proof of Deceased
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • PAN Card
  • Ration Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
Informant Documents
  • Aadhaar Card of informant
  • Relationship proof with deceased
  • Address proof of informant
For Delayed Registration
  • Non-availability certificate from Registrar
  • Self-affidavit on stamp paper
  • RDO / Magistrate order (after 1 year)
  • Supporting proof of death
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Register Death Online via CRS / MeeSeva

AP offers online death registration through the CRS portal and MeeSeva citizen service platform.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to crsorgi.gov.in or ap.meeseva.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Create an account with your mobile number and Aadhaar. Verify via OTP.
  3. Select Death Registration
    Navigate to Civil Supplies / Health → Death Registration.
  4. Fill the Application Form
    Enter all details:
    • Name, age, gender, date and place of death
    • Cause of death as per medical certificate
    • Informant details and address
    • Municipality / Gram Panchayat jurisdiction
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload MCCD, ID proof, and informant documents. File size: max 200 KB (JPG/PDF)
  6. Pay Fee & Submit
    MeeSeva service charge (~₹35) applies. Note the Reference Number from SMS.
  7. Verification
    Revenue Inspector / Village Secretary verifies within 7–15 working days.
Tip: Keep all original documents ready in case the Revenue Inspector visits for verification.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Register Death Offline at MeeSeva / Grama Sachivalayam

Visit the nearest MeeSeva Center, Municipality, or Grama Sachivalayam to register.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit MeeSeva / Municipal Office
    Working hours: Monday – Saturday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
  2. Collect Application Form
    Obtain the Death Registration Form from the operator / counter.
  3. Fill the Form
    Fill accurately with deceased's details, cause of death, informant info.
  4. Submit Documents & Get Receipt
    Submit form + photocopies. Carry originals for verification. Receive Acknowledgement Receipt.
  5. Verification by Village Secretary / RI
    Local authority verifies within 7–15 working days.
  6. Collect Death Certificate
    Collect from MeeSeva / Municipal office after approval. SMS notification will be sent.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days for timely registration.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application

Visit crsorgi.gov.in or MeeSeva to check status.

Download Certificate

Download the digitally signed death certificate from CRS portal once approved.

Legal Use

Use for insurance, property mutation, bank settlement, pension, and succession proceedings.

Grievance

Call 1800-425-2977 or visit spb.ap.gov.in.
